8.display: False — every square is a rectangle, but not every rectangle is a square.

13.display: Always — a quadrilateral with four right angles must have opposite sides equal and parallel, so it is always a rectangle (a square counts as a special rectangle).

14.display: A pair of parallel lines marked with arrows, and one line drawn perpendicular (at 90°) to one of them, correctly labelled.

15.display: No — e.g. a rhombus or a kite has equal sides but is not a rectangle unless its angles are also right angles.

16.display: Yes — a square has all the properties of a rectangle (four right angles, opposite sides equal and parallel) plus all four sides equal, so it is a special rectangle.

17.display: Sometimes — an isosceles or equilateral triangle has at least two equal sides, but a scalene triangle does not.

18.display: A rhombus has four equal sides but its angles are not usually 90°. A square has four equal sides AND four right angles (90° each).

19.display: False — a regular polygon must have both equal sides AND equal angles; if the angles differ, the shape is not regular.

21.display: A right-angle corner sketched with a small square marking the angle; perpendicular means two lines meet at a right angle (90°).

22.display: 6 sides and 6 vertices. A square has only 4 sides and 4 vertices, and its angles (90°) are different from a hexagon's interior angles.

24.display: A kite shape with two pairs of adjacent (touching) sides marked as equal.

25.display: Right-angled triangles. Yes, the two triangles are the same size and shape (congruent), since the diagonal splits the rectangle exactly in half.

26.display: All three sides are equal in length, and all three angles are equal, each measuring 60°.

27.display: Trapezium (or trapezoid) — one pair of parallel sides.

28.display: Your own always/sometimes/never question about triangles or quadrilaterals, with a correct answer.

29.display: A hexagon with six sides of different lengths — it is still a hexagon because it has exactly six straight sides, regardless of whether they are equal.

30.display: Always has a right angle: square, rectangle. Does not always have a right angle: rhombus, kite.
